scenario_title: Operation-order dependence: add then delete same RR in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates a single temporal-ordering choice inside one UPDATE: the server must decide whether to treat the section as a sequential script or as a batch with normalization/conflict handling. By preconditioning ord.campus.edu. A to be absent, then adding and deleting the exact same RR in one message, the scenario exercises the underspecified intra-message evaluation model while keeping the follow-up QUERY simple and within the touched name space.

group_1.behavior: Processes UPDATE terms sequentially; add then delete of the same RR yields no net change and succeeds.

group_2.behavior: Normalizes or revalidates the UPDATE as a batch and may reject or otherwise differ when it encounters contradictory same-message add/delete operations.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2–§3.4.3 define the meaning of add and delete operations but do not prescribe whether multiple UPDATE RRs must be applied strictly in message order or can be coalesced/revalidated as a batch. That leaves the intra-message conflict resolution model unspecified, so an add_rr followed by delete_rr of the same RR can produce different server behavior even without concurrency.
